introduction: This presentation explores our Q2 2024 strategy on leveraging customer feedback to enhance our online customer service. We will also review its impact on our response time and overall customer satisfaction.

subsections.heading: Overview of Q2 2024 Customer Feedback
subsections.content:
• In Q2 2024, Lee’s Market received over 2,000 customer feedback forms per week
• 65% of the feedback was related to in-store shopping experiences
• The feedback ranged across various store departments, including produce, bakery, and seafood
• 30% of responses pointed towards product quality and pricing
• Feedback forms were presented in multiple languages, including English, Mandarin, Korean and Vietnamese
• Over 70% of the customers used the digital feedback form available on the website
subsections.justification: Details customer feedback but doesn't specify how it was used to reduce response time for online inquiries. Focuses on general feedback statistics.

subsections.heading: Implementation of Feedback in Customer Service
subsections.content:
• The customer service team held weekly meetings to review and analyze feedback
• Feedback was categorized into different themes for clearer understanding
• Efforts were made to improve in-store experiences based on feedback
• The customer service team underwent training to handle feedback more effectively
• Customer service prioritized feedback that had the highest frequency
subsections.justification: Details the customer service team's handling of feedback but doesn't mention how it impacted online customer inquiry response time. Focuses on procedural information.

subsections.heading: Impact on Online Customer Inquiry Response Time
subsections.content:
• The average response time for online inquiries was 24 hours in Q2 2024
• There was a 10% increase in online customer inquiries in Q2 2024 compared to Q1 2024
• Lee’s Market hired additional customer service staff to handle the increased volume of inquiries
• The company integrated a new AI chatbot to assist with handling customer inquiries
• The new chatbot was responsible for handling 15% of all online inquiries
subsections.justification: Discusses changes to the online inquiry process but doesn't link these changes to customer feedback forms. Focuses on operational details.

conclusion: Through strategic use of customer feedback, we managed to significantly reduce our online inquiry response time. Moving forward, we must continue to leverage this feedback to improve our services and maintain customer satisfaction.
